summary:
The extraction procedure is complex due to proximity to critical structures; potential issues mainly revolve around local tissue trauma or displacement without significant risk to deeper bone structures as indicated by option A.
```
The summary focuses only on the two main aspects related directly back to the original requirements for this particular case scenario involving a distoangularly impacted lower wisdom tooth. ```plaintext

Extraction of disto-angular impacted third molars requires careful surgery near vital anatomical structures, with risks including localized tissue injury such as slippage into the lingual sulcus during removal.
Given these considerations, extracting an impacted third molar could lead primarily to minor displacements within accessible areas instead of more severe structural damages specified elsewhere among possible outcomes.
```
